title: NOC 11109 – Other Financial Officers | GTR Immigration
description: Other Financial Officers under NOC 11109 are highly sought-after professionals in Canada’s financial services sector. These specialists work across regulatory inspection, underwriting, trust administration, investigations, and mortgage brokerage—making them strong candidates for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).

classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree

duties: Professionals in this group may perform some or all of the following:
Review banks, insurers, trust companies, pension funds, and securities firms for regulatory compliance
Assess financial operations against governing legislation
Investigate unethical conduct or violations of securities and commodity laws
Analyze risk for mortgages, securities, and financing solutions
Negotiate financial terms with governments, corporations, or individuals
Administer estate, corporate, charitable, and personal trusts
Direct trust investments and manage income distribution
Evaluate clients’ financial profiles and property details
Negotiate mortgage products with lenders on behalf of clients
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in commerce, economics, business administration, or related fields
Industry training through institutions such as the Canadian Securities Institute or banking associations
Recognized designations such as CFA or CIM (may be required)
Accounting designations for examiners or inspectors in some roles
Provincial mortgage broker licensing in several provinces
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create 66,000 new positions for Other Financial Officers, while approximately 61,400 job seekers may enter the occupation. For professionals from India and other countries, this presents promising opportunities with the right immigration strategy.
labour_market_demand: Other Financial Officers remain in strong demand across banks, trust companies, investment firms, regulatory agencies, and government departments. Post-pandemic growth in financial compliance, lending, and wealth management continues to drive hiring needs nationwide.
Career mobility across public and private sectors
Favorable immigration prospects for internationally trained applicants
Annual earnings typically range from $41,600 to $120,300, depending on experience, region, and specialization.
Immigrating under NOC 11109 provides access to:
Secure employment in regulated financial institutions
Clear pathways to Canadian Permanent Residence (PR)
